verdict_reasoning: LYN is a Src-family tyrosine kinase anchored to the inner leaflet of the plasma membrane via N-terminal myristoylation/palmitoylation — canonically cytoplasmic-facing. However, like other SFKs, LYN has documented ecto-display on tumor cells (cancer ecto-kinase pool) and externalization during apoptosis/immunogenic cell death, providing a non-baseline outer-leaflet pool. No TM domain, no GPI, no stable TM-partner tethering at baseline. Lysosomal exocytosis n/a; no narrow germline/developmental restriction; no documented wash-resistant TM-partner anchor. The contextual call rests on cancer/cell-death-induced ecto-presentation.
predicted_key_uncertainty: Strength of evidence for LYN-specific tumor ecto-surface display is modest; primarily inferred from SFK-family convergent biology rather than LYN-specific surface proteomics.
